message:
🐛 Disable HTTP/2 on k8s API clients to fix hpack panic (#2256)

The Go HTTP/2 hpack encoder has a known bug where concurrent connections
trigger a panic: "id (N) <= evictCount (M)" in tables.go. This crashes
the backend process repeatedly on the vllm-d cluster.

Force HTTP/1.1 on all outbound Kubernetes API connections by setting
NextProtos=["http/1.1"] on every rest.Config before client creation.
Applied at all client creation points:
- MultiClusterClient.getClient() (kubernetes clientset)
- MultiClusterClient.GetDynamicClient() (dynamic client)
- InClusterConfig initialization
- Prometheus proxy transport
- kubectl test connection

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@ import (
 	"time"
 
 	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/agent/protocol"
+	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/k8s"
 	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
 	"k8s.io/client-go/rest"
 	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/clientcmd"
@@ -572,6 +573,7 @@ func (k *KubectlProxy) TestClusterConnection(req TestConnectionRequest) (*TestCo
 		cfg.TLSClientConfig.CAData = caBytes
 	}
 	cfg.TLSClientConfig.Insecure = req.SkipTLSVerify
+	k8s.DisableHTTP2(cfg)
 
 	client, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(cfg)
 	if err != nil {
